title: Using a Service to Expose Your App — Step 3: Deleting a service
text:
To delete Services you can use the
delete service
subcommand. Labels can be used
also here:
kubectl delete service -l
app
=
kubernetes-bootcamp
Confirm that the Service is gone:
kubectl get services
This confirms that our Service was removed. To confirm that route is not exposed
anymore you can
curl
the previously exposed IP and port:
curl http://
"
$(
minikube ip
)
:
$NODE_PORT
"
This proves that the application is not reachable anymore from outside of the cluster.
You can confirm that the app is still running with a
curl
from inside the pod:
kubectl
exec
-ti
$POD_NAME
-- curl http://localhost:8080
We see here that the application is up. This is because the Deployment is managing
the application. To shut down the application, you would need to delete the Deployment
as well.
